key_insight: WeldMamba uses physics-guided spatiotemporal state-space modeling to predict future weld-pool regions from past images, process signals, and wire-state data for lookahead segmentation.
engineering_relevance: By anticipating molten pool, keyhole, and wire geometry up to 500 ms ahead, the method can support lower-latency closed-loop control in laser wire-feed welding.
method: Fuses coaxial grayscale video with welding parameters and aligned electrical signals.
Uses patch-level temporal state-space modeling plus horizon-conditioned latent prediction to forecast future features.
Adds motion-aware decoding and auxiliary geometric losses (SDF, temporal consistency, distillation) to sharpen region boundaries.
evidence_signal: On a 43-sequence laser welding dataset, the method achieved 74.63% mIoU at a 500 ms lookahead, with ablations showing temporal history, patch-level state-space modeling, and keyhole motion awareness were most important.
watch_item: The approach depends on tightly aligned multimodal sensing and may be sensitive to domain shift, synchronization errors, or missing signals in real welding setups.
paper_title: Physics-Guided Spatiotemporal State Space Modeling for Lookahead Molten Pool Segmentation in Laser Wire-Feed Welding
